category_title: Students expressing school-related frustration through anger and peer-interaction difficulties
minimal_context: The tutor is showing Patrick photos of a screech owl in the classroom as he prepares to transition home at the end of the day.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Patrick, here are the photos of the screech owl for you to look at.

STUDENT: School is dumb. I want to go home.

TUTOR: Your mother just arrived to pick you up.

STUDENT: (Pushes the photos away) I'm mad!

TUTOR: It is time to transition and go home now.

STUDENT: (Kicks the furniture in frustration) I'm not doing it! Leave me alone!

options.A: Express empathy towards him using verbalizations like "What's the matter? I know, I know" and "Did you not want to leave the classroom?"
options.B: Ask Patrick if he would like to take the photo of the screech owl home to draw a picture of it, then bring it back tomorrow.
options.C: Immediately remove all demands and the screech owl photos for 60 seconds before attempting to continue the transition.
options.D: Give Patrick a verbal warning regarding his behavior before proceeding with the transition to go home.
